stream_name: Methodological moat + governance decisions + data provenance

audit_method.approach: Every moat sub-claim traced to a specific key in moat_comparison.json; governance decisions traced to signed statements + repo commit paths.
audit_method.raw_json_carry: moat_comparison keys are preserved verbatim in attributes so a downstream ingestor can rebuild any dimension without re-summarising.
audit_method.governance_ground_truth: PATH A formula + 8D remediation status are copied from the user's canonical governance record (2026-04-28).

entities.name: Methodological moat — full comparison record (anchor)
entities.what_it_solves: Aggregate anchor for the full moat_comparison.json record; sibling to the per-dimension entities.
entities.how_it_solves: Preserves top-level keys of moat_comparison.json + the full document_purpose + provenance + honest_moat_assessment blocks so consumers can walk them without re-summarising.
entities.why_it_exists: The moat comparison is CrisPRO's positioning artefact; cross-refs to the moat 'as a whole' land here rather than picking an arbitrary dimension.

attributes.document_purpose: Compare CrisPRO's methodological 'moat' against Bevilacqua 2026's published approach + adjacent literature, with every claim traceable to PDF, transcript receipts, or paper references.

primary_source.paper: Bevilacqua R, Gasperini P, Cantore T, et al. Therapeutic vulnerabilities exposed by the 9p21 loss identified through multiparametric drug screening inform rational combination strategies.
primary_source.retrieval_method: Full PDF read (16 pages) via Read tool on /mnt/user-uploads/s41698-026-01434-w.pdf, 2026-06-24
primary_source.code_availability: Not publicly available; available on reasonable request from corresponding authors per paper Code Availability statement

provenance.verification_status: Every leaf below is traceable to a specific page/figure/table of the source PDF or to a verifiable transcript receipt. No claim retained from prior generated JSONs unless re-verified.
audit_corrections_applied: MTAP-loss prevalence in TCGA MIBC = 26% (paper text, Discussion §1, cBioPortal computation by the authors). 22% in PLAN.md was the older Robertson 2017 CDKN2A-alone figure for MIBC; both retained, distinguished.

Phenotypic features = 12 (verbatim, Results §Phenotype-based hit selection: 'For each object (i.e., cell or nucleus), 12 phenotypic features...'). PLAN.md had hedged this to 'feature count not in retrieved supplementary'; PDF resolves it definitively to 12.
SynergyFinder version = 3.1 (verbatim, Fig 3 legend). Reference 21 (Ianevski 2022) is the SynergyFinder 3.0 paper; the authors used the 3.1 release derived from the same software lineage.
ATR inhibitor name verbatim = VX970 (no hyphen, no 'berzosertib'). Paper uses VX970 throughout.

Compound library composition: 2,349 total = 349 (Selleck Anti-cancer) + 958 (MicroSource Spectrum known drugs in human therapy) + 629 (natural products) + 343 (biological activity at experimental level) + 70 (approved for agricultural use only). Numerical sum = 2,349.

PDO clinical context: BlCa197 = 54-year-old female, high-grade MIBC, mixed urothelial+sarcomatoid, neoadjuvant cisplatin/gemcitabine + radical cystectomy. TN-001 = 53-year-old male, high-grade MIBC, mixed urothelial+sarcomatoid, MVAC combination + radical cystectomy.
AZD7762 and MK-1775 z-scores from primary screen now stated verbatim: AZD7762 WT z=-3.60 / 3KO z=-5.06; MK-1775 WT z=-1.12 / 3KO z=-2.06.

MRTX1719 axis bug in repo: pharmacologic_analyzer.py:66 maps 'mrtx1719' -> 'wrn'. Correct axis per Bev2026 §Methods +  Engstrom 2023 ref 7 = PRMT5 (MTA-cooperative). Flag in DH JSON; no code fix in this task per user lock #4.

attributes.definition_of_moat: Defensible methodological features that distinguish CrisPRO's approach from established or emerging competitors. Moat ≠ moral superiority; moat = features competitors cannot easily replicate without comparable infrastructure, expertise, or data access.

where_CrisPRO_genuinely_outperforms: Computational falsification at scale (PARP1 axis in MBD4-LOF context demonstrated; methodology generalizable)
Systematic confound stress-testing within single dataset (MSI + TP53 + LOO + lineage)
Open code + artifact provenance with full-precision statistical values
Cross-axis evidence matrix that survives compaction (canonical artifact frozen 2026-04-05)
where_Bev2026_outperforms: Cellular validation in isogenic pairs + cross-tumor + PDOs (gold-standard biological evidence)
Bi-directional MTAP editing (rescue + KO) as in-cell falsification mechanism
Wet-lab synergy measurement (SynergyFinder 3.1 HSA scores with 95% CI)
honest_moat_assessment.complementarity_opportunity: CrisPRO + Bev2026 lab = computational triage + cellular validation. CrisPRO ranks candidate PDOs by biomarker score → Demichelis/Kruithof-de Julio lab validates the top-N in wet lab. Each side does what it cannot do alone.
fix_required_before_outreach: MRTX1719 axis-mapping bug in pharmacologic_analyzer.py:66 (PRMT5 vs WRN) — flagged in DH JSON and CVP JSON. Recommended pre-outreach fix.
PRMT5_MTAP_COOPERATIVE axis missing from CandidateAxis enum (currently 7 axes; this would be the 8th).

n=942 prose value (manuscript Abstract/Table/Fig 2 caption) does not reproduce from canonical_atr_wee1_rerun.json — user's own audit report flags this as an unresolved item; affects only internal CrisPRO manuscript, not outreach material.

comparative_summary_table.Bev2026: Low N high quality (paired isogenic n=6)
comparative_summary_table.CrisPRO: High N (n=914 WT, n=14 LOF, n=1498 comparator)
Formal enum, MRTX1719 bug at HEAD bfd6d11f
No primary PDO; in-silico triage offer
Open code + artifact provenance + 10 unit tests
Not currently performed; offers axis pairing ranking only

moat_caveats.caveat_title: Reproducibility-as-moat is contingent on unresolved governance items
moat_caveats.scope: Applies to all 'CrisPRO ADVANTAGE on reproducibility / code transparency' claims in this document (Dimensions 3, 7, and the honest_moat_assessment block).

issue_1_pc02_quarantine.claim_at_risk: honest_moat_assessment.where_CrisPRO_genuinely_outperforms includes: 'Open code + artifact provenance with full-precision statistical values'
issue_1_pc02_quarantine.provenance_status: PARTIALLY VERIFIED — the existence of PC-02 as a quarantined item is confirmed in /mnt/results/execution_trace/NOTES.md verbatim line: 'QUARANTINED: DL-07 Figure 2 (DDR 0.983), LATIFY deltas, PC-02'. The full expansion of what PC-02 means ('permanently downgraded until retroactive_prediction_run.py --trial-vectors reproduces documented deltas cleanly') is sourced from AI Notes memory only, not from a tool output in this session's transcript.
issue_1_pc02_quarantine.downgrade_to_apply: The 'reproducibility' moat claim in Dimension 7 is degraded until PC-02 is resolved. The statement 'Open code + artifact provenance' remains true at the file-existence level, but the assertion that those artifacts represent verified-reproducible work is not yet substantiated.
issue_1_pc02_quarantine.what_this_means_for_outreach: Demichelis/Kruithof-de Julio outreach should NOT lead with 'reproducible canonical artifacts' as a differentiator until the project's own retroactive reproduction passes. Lead with the falsification methodology (PARP1 Mann-Whitney) and stress-test structure (MSI/TP53/LOO/lineage) — those are confirmed by full-precision values in transcript indices 991/1010.

issue_2_n942_vs_n914_discrepancy.claim_at_risk: moat_comparison.json Dimension 3 stress-test breakdown uses n_WT=914 (canonical_atr_wee1_rerun.json 2026-04-05 Methods_no_somatic_MBD4 rule).
issue_2_n942_vs_n914_discrepancy.provenance_status: VERIFIED that the MBD4 manuscript branch is named 'feat/mbd4-rxiv-manuscript-942-baseline' — see transcript index 970 user message verbatim: 'https://github.com/fjkiani/Synthetic-Lethality/tree/feat/mbd4-rxiv-manuscript-942-baseline/00-mbd4-manuscript/mbd4_parp_response'. The branch name implies the manuscript prose was anchored on n=942, while the canonical artifact frozen 2026-04-05 yields n=914 under Methods_no_somatic_MBD4 rule. The 28-line discrepancy is real and unresolved at the project level.
issue_2_n942_vs_n914_discrepancy.specific_uncertainty: (a) Whether n=942 is the alternate rule (includes MBD4 non-LOF somatic mutants → reaches n=922 per WT_rule_canonical alternate path, not 942 — so n=942 may be yet a third rule). (b) Which N is the manuscript source-of-truth. (c) Whether downstream deltas/p-values change materially under the different rule.
issue_2_n942_vs_n914_discrepancy.downgrade_to_apply: The Dimension 3 framing 'n=914 (Methods rule)' is correct for what we cite, but moat-comparison should NOT claim 'CrisPRO has reconciled sample-size definitions across the canonical pipeline' — that reconciliation has not been demonstrated.
issue_2_n942_vs_n914_discrepancy.what_this_means_for_outreach: If a reviewer asks 'why does your branch say 942 but your audit values use 914?', the project lead needs a one-sentence answer before that conversation happens.

issue_3_repo_state_temporal_qualificatio.claim_at_risk: MRTX1719 bug at pharmacologic_analyzer.py:66 (at HEAD bfd6d11f, branch fix/mbd4-atr-strong-tier).
issue_3_repo_state_temporal_qualificatio.provenance_status: VERIFIED at last inspection — HEAD bfd6d11f confirmed in transcript via earlier worker-mounted git operations. However, the worker that performed that inspection has been terminated. Current live state of the bug is UNKNOWN.
issue_3_repo_state_temporal_qualificatio.downgrade_to_apply: Bug claim must be qualified with 'as of last verified commit bfd6d11f' — not 'current production state'.
issue_3_repo_state_temporal_qualificatio.what_this_means_for_outreach: If MRTX1719 bug is referenced in any external-facing communication, it should be qualified as 'at the time of audit' to allow the user to address the bug between audit and outreach without invalidating the comparison.

what_this_caveat_block_does_NOT_change: The 4 stress tests + PARP1 falsification arm with full-precision values remain unaffected — those numbers exist in canonical_atr_wee1_rerun.json and were quoted verbatim from transcript indices 991/1010 + user message line 12288 (i=11560).
Bev2026 verbatim quotes with PDF page citations remain unaffected.
The methodological framing (computational falsification + stress-test stratification) is the strongest part of the moat and is independently verifiable from the canonical receipts.
what_this_caveat_block_DOES_change: Tone of Dimension 7 (Reproducibility infrastructure): the CrisPRO ADVANTAGE label remains structurally accurate (open code vs Bev2026's code-withheld) but the IMPLEMENTATION advantage is degraded.
honest_moat_assessment.where_CrisPRO_genuinely_outperforms: the 4th bullet ('Cross-axis evidence matrix that survives compaction') is unaffected; the 1st-3rd bullets remain accurate.
fix_required_before_outreach: added PC-02 resolution + n=942/n=914 reconciliation alongside existing items.
moat_caveats.memory_vs_transcript_provenance_principl: Following 2026-06-24 system reminder: claims sourced from AI Notes memory only are tagged as 'MEMORY-SOURCED' here; claims confirmed by direct tool output in /mnt/results/execution_trace/transcript.jsonl are tagged 'TRANSCRIPT-VERIFIED'. PC-02 existence = TRANSCRIPT-VERIFIED (NOTES.md). PC-02 expansion = MEMORY-SOURCED (no transcript confirmation found via rg -F 'PC-02' /mnt/results/execution_trace/transcript.jsonl in this session).

corrections_applied: Dim 1: Added 'PDF Introduction page 2' source location for 'Unlike cell viability screenings' verbatim quote.
Dim 3: Restored full-precision values for all 4 stress tests + primary endpoint (Δ to 16 sig figs, p-values exact, Cohen's d exact) — matches canonical_atr_wee1_rerun.json frozen 2026-04-05.
Dim 5: Added 3 verbatim Bev2026 quotes with page citations: Results page 5 header, Ref 7 Engstrom 2023, Ref 8 Pettus 2025.
Dim 6: Added 3 verbatim Bev2026 quotes with page citations: Methods page 14 PDO clinical history, Methods page 14 bi-directional editing, Results page 8 rescue finding.
Dim 8 (NEW): Synergy quantification — added Fig 3 caption verbatim with page 7 source. SynergyFinder 3.1 vs ref 21 (3.0) distinction noted.

Dim 9 (NEW): MTAP-loss prevalence attribution — 3 verbatim quotes (Intro p.2, Results p.2, Discussion p.12) explicitly disambiguating 26% (Bev2026 MTAP-codeletion) vs 22% (Robertson 2017 CDKN2A-alone).
Removed any implicit claim that CrisPRO 'verified' or 'reproduced' the 26% figure — it is Bev2026's own analysis.
Audit checked attribution drift: 0 concerns. All quantitative claims trace to verified transcript receipts or verified PDF text. Repo state qualified with 'HEAD bfd6d11f'.
claims_remaining_in_quarantine: None in moat_comparison.json. All quantitative claims either cite verbatim verbatim or are explicitly labeled as inferences.
claims_NOT_made_in_this_document: Wet-lab competitor comparisons (Mirati Phase II, Agios Phase I, etc.) — explicitly out of scope per competitor_landscape_not_assessed_this_session block.
Specific resolution recommendation for MRTX1719 bug — only the bug location and correct mapping per Bev2026 are stated; whether to fix or not remains the user's call per lock #4.
audit_corrections_2026_06_24.full_precision_audit_pass: All canonical receipt values present at full precision: 0.7324529879649893 (delta), 0.05564297952485164 (AUC delta), 1.0692019443383756 (TP53 delta), 0.045165724128583974 (LOO max p), n=619 (TP53 strat WT).
caveat_blocks_added: MOAT-CAV-001 (reproducibility-as-moat contingent on PC-02 + n=942/n=914 + repo-state-temporal qualification)

entities.name: Primary discovery design — multi-modal vs single-modal
entities.what_it_solves: Primary discovery design — multi-modal vs single-modal
entities.how_it_solves: 7-modality candidate axis enum at api/services/synthetic_lethality/v3/multimodal/models.py:122: CYTIDINE_ANALOGS, PARP_INHIBITORS, ATR_WEE1, WRN, IMMUNOTHERAPY, PKMYT1, CUSTOM. EvidenceRow combines GDSC pharmacology + DepMap CRISPR + literature receipt + expression + in vitro + in vivo + clinical cells.
entities.why_it_exists: Comparator stance (PARITY (different dimensions, similar philosophy). Bev2026 axes are biological readouts within one screen; CrisPRO axes are evidence modalities across data types.): Bev2026 does 'Two parallel hit-selection axes from same screen — viability z-score (Fig 1E) AND morphology Pearson correlation (Fig 2B). Independent nomination paths catch gemcitabine which the viability axis missed.'

attributes.moat_status: PARITY (different dimensions, similar philosophy). Bev2026 axes are biological readouts within one screen; CrisPRO axes are evidence modalities across data types.
attributes.Bev2026_implementation: Two parallel hit-selection axes from same screen — viability z-score (Fig 1E) AND morphology Pearson correlation (Fig 2B). Independent nomination paths catch gemcitabine which the viability axis missed.
attributes.Bev2026_approach_verbatim: Unlike cell viability screenings that provide only binary outcomes (live or dead), multiparametric drug screenings can capture complex cellular responses, offering insights into drug mechanisms of action, types of cell death, and molecular responses.
attributes.Bev2026_approach_verbatim_source: Bev2026 PDF, Introduction (page 2), final paragraph before Results
attributes.verification_status: Verbatim quote confirmed by direct PDF read of pages 1-3, 2026-06-24. Originally from Introduction (not Discussion as the dimension framing might imply).

entities.name: Falsification arm methodology
entities.what_it_solves: Falsification arm methodology
entities.how_it_solves: PARP1 expression Mann-Whitney falsification arm: MBD4-LOF vs comparator (n=19/1498), median LOF 6.770 vs comparator 6.656, p=0.605 (two-sided). Sanity check: pan-cancer PARP1×PARPi ρ=-0.416, p=1.36e-21, n=481. Verifies that PARP-trapping mechanism is NOT operative in MBD4-LOF and pivots toward ATR axis as recommended therapy.
entities.why_it_exists: Encoded gap between CrisPRO methodology and Bev2026.

attributes.moat_status: COMPLEMENTARY. Bev2026's wet-lab 2KO falsification is gold-standard but constrained to one gene at a time. CrisPRO's in-silico Mann-Whitney falsification scales across 18,000+ genes × multiple axes.
attributes.verification_status: Bev2026 from PDF §Results + Supp Fig 2D. CrisPRO from user-provided 'precise numbers' message transcript line 12288 (verbatim).

raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_approach: 2KO clones (CDKN2A/2B deleted, MTAP retained) used as in-cell falsification arm. If sensitivity is MTAP-driven, 2KO should NOT respond to PRMT5/MAT2A inhibitors → confirmed (Supp Fig 2B, 2D).
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_evidence_quality: Strong - actual cellular falsification in same assay system.
raw_dimension_record.CrisPRO_approach: PARP1 expression Mann-Whitney falsification arm: MBD4-LOF vs comparator (n=19/1498), median LOF 6.770 vs comparator 6.656, p=0.605 (two-sided). Sanity check: pan-cancer PARP1×PARPi ρ=-0.416, p=1.36e-21, n=481. Verifies that PARP-trapping mechanism is NOT operative in MBD4-LOF and pivots toward ATR axis as recommended therapy.
raw_dimension_record.CrisPRO_evidence_quality: In-silico falsification using public DepMap expression data + pan-cancer pharmacology Spearman correlation. Differs from Bev2026 in being computational not cellular, but enables falsification of competing hypotheses at scale across the full gene-axis matrix.
raw_dimension_record.moat_status: COMPLEMENTARY. Bev2026's wet-lab 2KO falsification is gold-standard but constrained to one gene at a time. CrisPRO's in-silico Mann-Whitney falsification scales across 18,000+ genes × multiple axes.
raw_dimension_record.verification_status: Bev2026 from PDF §Results + Supp Fig 2D. CrisPRO from user-provided 'precise numbers' message transcript line 12288 (verbatim).

entities.name: Stress testing / confound robustness
entities.what_it_solves: Stress testing / confound robustness
entities.how_it_solves: See moat_comparison.json for the full crispro-vs-comparator table.
entities.why_it_exists: Encoded gap between CrisPRO methodology and Bev2026.

attributes.moat_status: CrisPRO ADVANTAGE for systematic confound testing across stratifiers. Bev2026 validates across systems; CrisPRO validates across statistical stratifiers within one dataset. Both are necessary, but CrisPRO's approach can be applied PRE-publication to triage hypotheses.
attributes.verification_status: Bev2026 from PDF §Results + Supp Fig 1A, 1F, 6A. CrisPRO from canonical_atr_wee1_rerun.json frozen 2026-04-05, full-precision JSON in transcript indices 991/1010 + user-provided 'precise numbers' message line 12288.

raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_approach: (a) Test in 2 independent isogenic clone pairs per cell line (Fig 1F, 1G) to exclude clone-selection artifact. (b) Test in T24 (second cell line) and PDOs (2 patient sources) to exclude cell-line artifact. (c) Test in pancreatic adenocarcinoma + mesothelioma to exclude lineage artifact.
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_evidence_quality: Strong cellular validation across systems but no formal stratifier analysis with explicit n breakdowns.
CrisPRO_approach_with_receipts: Stress #1 (MSI purge): exclude ModelSubtypeFeatures containing 'MSI' from both arms. Effect STRENGTHENS: n_LOF=10/n_WT=906, Δ=-0.9095277046357615, p=0.015328932966132268, d=-0.6227047947387747. Rules out: MSI-H as confounder.

Stress #2 (TP53 stratification): MBD4-LOF∩TP53-mut vs MBD4-WT(Methods)∩TP53-mut. Effect is LARGEST: n_LOF=11/n_WT=619, LN_IC50 Δ=-1.0692019443383756, p=0.003002668797799231, d=-0.7404782024497254. AUC Δ=-0.08694390659421358, p=0.0008734372333767206, d=-0.8888045345020856. Rules out: TP53 dependency; relevant because >95% of HGSOC is TP53-mutant.
Stress #3 (Leave-one-out × 14): Max p across 14 iterations = 0.045165724128583974. All 14/14 iterations p<0.05. Rules out: Single-line driver.

Stress #4 (Non-bowel lineage): n_LOF=9/n_WT=873, Δ=-0.8706814558991984, p=0.02533035508952329, d=-0.5988100454283156. Rules out: Bowel-lineage artifact (bowel-only n_LOF=5, p=0.12638007986845198 underpowered).
Primary endpoint (Methods_no_somatic_MBD4 rule, ceralasertib LN_IC50): n_LOF=14, n_WT=914, Δ=-0.7324529879649893, p=0.021484496737088882, d=-0.5032867186922607.
raw_dimension_record.moat_status: CrisPRO ADVANTAGE for systematic confound testing across stratifiers. Bev2026 validates across systems; CrisPRO validates across statistical stratifiers within one dataset. Both are necessary, but CrisPRO's approach can be applied PRE-publication to triage hypotheses.
raw_dimension_record.verification_status: Bev2026 from PDF §Results + Supp Fig 1A, 1F, 6A. CrisPRO from canonical_atr_wee1_rerun.json frozen 2026-04-05, full-precision JSON in transcript indices 991/1010 + user-provided 'precise numbers' message line 12288.
raw_dimension_record.CrisPRO_receipts_source: canonical_atr_wee1_rerun.json frozen 2026-04-05. Full content retrievable from transcript indices 991/1010 (raw.githubusercontent.com/fjkiani/Synthetic-Lethality/feat/mbd4-rxiv-manuscript-942-baseline/00-mbd4-manuscript/mbd4_parp_response/artifacts/canonical_atr_wee1_rerun_20260405/canonical_atr_wee1_rerun.json) + user-provided 'precise numbers' message at transcript line 12288 (i=11560).

entities.why_it_exists: Encoded gap between CrisPRO methodology and Bev2026.

attributes.moat_status: DIFFERENT KIND OF STATISTICAL POWER. Bev2026 has high biological replicate quality (n=6 per condition, paired isogenic) with low N. CrisPRO has high N (1000+ cell lines) with single-readout pharmacology. Best practice combines both.
attributes.verification_status: Bev2026 from PDF figure legends. CrisPRO from canonical receipts.

raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_n: 2 isogenic clone pairs (HT1197 + T24) + 2 PDOs (TN-001 + BlCa197) + 2 cross-tumor models (SW1990 + SPC111). Total isogenic readout systems: 4 cell line pairs + 2 PDO pairs = 6 paired systems.
raw_dimension_record.CrisPRO_n_for_MBD4_ATR_axis: n_LOF=14, n_WT=914 (Methods rule). Plus DepMap CRISPR n=1186 lines for gene-effect dependency.
raw_dimension_record.CrisPRO_comparator_pool_for_PARP1_expres: n=19/1498 (broader because expression data covers more lines than drug-screen data)
raw_dimension_record.moat_status: DIFFERENT KIND OF STATISTICAL POWER. Bev2026 has high biological replicate quality (n=6 per condition, paired isogenic) with low N. CrisPRO has high N (1000+ cell lines) with single-readout pharmacology. Best practice combines both.
raw_dimension_record.verification_status: Bev2026 from PDF figure legends. CrisPRO from canonical receipts.

entities.name: Axis taxonomy and target-class mapping
entities.what_it_solves: Axis taxonomy and target-class mapping
entities.how_it_solves: Formal enum CandidateAxis at api/services/synthetic_lethality/v3/multimodal/models.py:122 with 7 axes. Drug-to-axis mapping table at pharmacologic_analyzer.py:66.
entities.why_it_exists: Encoded gap between CrisPRO methodology and Bev2026.

attributes.moat_status: Bev2026 ADVANTAGE on biology (correct axis assignment). CrisPRO has the framework but a fixable bug in the mapping.

raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_approach: Paper introduces 'rational drug combinations' across 4 mechanism axes: nucleoside analogs (cytarabine, gemcitabine), antifolates (MTX), PRMT5 inhibitors (MRTX1719) + MAT2A inhibitors (AG-270), ATR/CHK1/WEE1 inhibitors (VX970, AZD7762, MK-1775). No formal enum.
raw_dimension_record.CrisPRO_approach: Formal enum CandidateAxis at api/services/synthetic_lethality/v3/multimodal/models.py:122 with 7 axes. Drug-to-axis mapping table at pharmacologic_analyzer.py:66.

CrisPRO_known_gap.correct_mapping_per_Bev2026_and_Engstrom: 'mrtx1719': 'prmt5_mtap_cooperative' (a new axis that does not yet exist in the CandidateAxis enum)
CrisPRO_known_gap.evidence_strength: TRIPLE-SOURCE: Bev2026 §Results + Methods (MRTX1719 = PRMT5 inhibitor); Engstrom 2023 Cancer Discov 13:2412-2431 (Bev2026 ref 7) is the originating paper defining MRTX1719 as MTA-cooperative PRMT5 inhibitor; Pettus 2025 J Med Chem 68:6932-6954 (Bev2026 ref 8) confirms MTA-cooperative PRMT5 axis for AMG 193.
CrisPRO_known_gap.remediation_required: Add PRMT5_MTAP_COOPERATIVE to CandidateAxis enum; remap mrtx1719 and add AMG 193 / pemrametostat to same axis. Out of scope per user lock #4.
CrisPRO_known_gap.impact_on_demichelis_outreach: Demichelis lab will immediately notice this bug if shown the current taxonomy. Pre-outreach fix recommended.

raw_dimension_record.moat_status: Bev2026 ADVANTAGE on biology (correct axis assignment). CrisPRO has the framework but a fixable bug in the mapping.
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_evidence_verbatim: First, we investigated the activity of the MAT2A inhibitor AG-270 and the PRMT5 inhibitor MRTX1719, which selectively binds to PRMT5 complexed with MTA, to define the ideal dose for a clear genotype-specific effect when administered alone in our cell models.
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_evidence_verbatim_source: Bev2026 PDF, Results section header 'Cytarabine synergizes with PRMT5 and MAT2A inhibitors selectively in bladder cancer cells with MTAP loss' (page 5, first paragraph)
raw_dimension_record.Engstrom_2023_verbatim: Engstrom, L. D. et al. MRTX1719 is an MTA-cooperative PRMT5 inhibitor that exhibits synthetic lethality in preclinical models and patients with MTAP-deleted cancer. Cancer Discov. 13, 2412–2431 (2023).
raw_dimension_record.Pettus_2025_verbatim: Pettus, L. H. et al. Discovery of AMG 193, an MTA-cooperative PRMT5 inhibitor for the treatment of MTAP-deleted cancers. J. Med. Chem. 68, 6932–6954 (2025).

entities.name: Patient-derived organoid (PDO) deployment
entities.what_it_solves: Patient-derived organoid (PDO) deployment
entities.how_it_solves: No primary PDO data — relies on DepMap CCLE cell lines (n=1186) + GDSC2 drug screens. Public-data dependent.
entities.why_it_exists: Encoded gap between CrisPRO methodology and Bev2026.

attributes.moat_status: Bev2026 ADVANTAGE on translational relevance. CrisPRO can offer pre-screening prioritization to Demichelis/Kruithof-de Julio PDO biobanks before they commit organoid resource.

raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_approach: Two PDOs from Inselspital Bern (BlCa197) + Spitalzentrum Biel (TN-001), both high-grade MIBC mixed urothelial/sarcomatoid. Bi-directional editing: TN-001 doxycycline-inducible MTAP rescue + BlCa197 MTAP-KO induction. CellTiter-Glo 3D readout. n=3 biological replicates × 2 technical replicates per drug condition.
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_validation: MTAP-OE in 3KO PDO RESCUES MRTX1719 activity; MTAP-KO in WT PDO INCREASES MRTX1719 activity. Bi-directional rescue is a powerful validation.
raw_dimension_record.CrisPRO_approach: No primary PDO data — relies on DepMap CCLE cell lines (n=1186) + GDSC2 drug screens. Public-data dependent.
raw_dimension_record.moat_status: Bev2026 ADVANTAGE on translational relevance. CrisPRO can offer pre-screening prioritization to Demichelis/Kruithof-de Julio PDO biobanks before they commit organoid resource.
raw_dimension_record.complementary_value: CrisPRO's biomarker-stratification triage + Kruithof-de Julio's TOR organoid bank = mutually beneficial pipeline.
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_PDO_clinical_history_verbatim: PDO lines, TN-001 and BlCa197, were generated from specimens obtained from patients who underwent cystectomy at the Inselspital, University Hospital, in Bern, or Spitalzentrum Biel, respectively. Specifically, BlCa197 cells were isolated from a 54-year-old female patient with high-grade MIBC with mixed urothelial and sarcomatoid histology. This patient underwent neoadjuvant cisplatin/gemcitabine treatment followed by radical cystectomy. TN-001 was obtained from a 53-year-old male patient diagnosed with high-grade MIBC with mixed urothelial and sarcomatoid histology. This patient received MVAC combination followed by radical cystectomy.
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_PDO_clinical_history_source: Bev2026 PDF, Methods section 'Clinical details of patient-derived organoid lines' (page 14)
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_bidirectional_editing_verbatim: To obtain TN-001 PDOs with doxycycline-inducible MTAP overexpression, the MTAP gene was cloned into pCW57.1 (Addgene #41393), which was then used to transduce TN-001 cells. For MTAP knockout in BlCa197 PDOs, MTAP sgRNA sequence was cloned into a lentiCRISPR_v1 plasmid as previously described, which was then used to transduce BlCa197 cells.
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_PDO_rescue_finding_verbatim: MTAP overexpression in TN-001 organoids rescues MRTX1719 activity, while MTAP KO selectively increases MRTX1719 activity in BlCa197 (Supplementary Fig. 7A).
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_PDO_rescue_finding_source: Bev2026 PDF, Results 'Combination of cytarabine and VX970 with MRTX1719 is effective in BLCA patient-derived organoids' (page 8)

entities.name: Synergy quantification methodology
entities.what_it_solves: Synergy quantification methodology
entities.how_it_solves: CrisPRO does not currently perform wet-lab synergy quantification — relies on GDSC2 single-agent IC50 / AUC + DepMap CRISPR dependency for axis ranking.
entities.why_it_exists: Encoded gap between CrisPRO methodology and Bev2026.

attributes.moat_status: Bev2026 ADVANTAGE on wet-lab synergy measurement methodology. CrisPRO can offer in-silico drug-combination ranking via GDSC2 axis pairing (e.g., ATR-inhibitor × cytidine-analog combination predictions for MBD4-LOF), but does not generate primary synergy scores.
attributes.Bev2026_approach_verbatim: Synergy was calculated with two models (HSA and Bliss) using SynergyFinder 3.1, and HSA synergy scores and summary synergy scores with 95% confidence interval for the CCK-8 experiments are shown.

raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_approach_verbatim: Synergy was calculated with two models (HSA and Bliss) using SynergyFinder 3.1, and HSA synergy scores and summary synergy scores with 95% confidence interval for the CCK-8 experiments are shown.
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_software_note: Paper uses SynergyFinder 3.1, but cites reference 21 (Ianevski et al. 2022 Nucleic Acids Res 50:W739-W743) which is the SynergyFinder 3.0 publication. The 3.1 release apparently postdates the original publication and is referenced by version number only in the figure caption.
raw_dimension_record.CrisPRO_approach: CrisPRO does not currently perform wet-lab synergy quantification — relies on GDSC2 single-agent IC50 / AUC + DepMap CRISPR dependency for axis ranking.
raw_dimension_record.moat_status: Bev2026 ADVANTAGE on wet-lab synergy measurement methodology. CrisPRO can offer in-silico drug-combination ranking via GDSC2 axis pairing (e.g., ATR-inhibitor × cytidine-analog combination predictions for MBD4-LOF), but does not generate primary synergy scores.
raw_dimension_record.complementary_value: If CrisPRO computational ranking nominates a combination, Bev2026's published HSA/Bliss methodology with 95% CI is the reproducible gold-standard for wet-lab validation.

verbatim_evidence.quote: Synergy was calculated with two models (HSA and Bliss) using SynergyFinder 3.1, and HSA synergy scores and summary synergy scores with 95% confidence interval for the CCK-8 experiments are shown.
verbatim_evidence.file: Bev2026 PDF (external, not in this KB corpus)
verbatim_evidence.note: Verbatim quote from Bev2026 as reproduced in moat_comparison.json; verification_status:

entities.name: Source of MTAP-loss MIBC prevalence statistic
entities.what_it_solves: Source of MTAP-loss MIBC prevalence statistic
entities.how_it_solves: Reproduces and cites Bev2026's 26% figure for MTAP/CDKN2A/CDKN2B co-deletion. The 22% figure (CDKN2A-alone in MIBC, Robertson 2017) is also cited as a complementary endpoint when MTAP-status is not specified. CrisPRO has NOT independently reproduced either number — both are taken from external citations.
entities.why_it_exists: Encoded gap between CrisPRO methodology and Bev2026.

attributes.moat_status: NEUTRAL — both numbers are Bev2026 + Robertson/Lindskrog original findings. CrisPRO cites them appropriately.
attributes.verification_status: All three verbatim quotes confirmed by direct PDF read 2026-06-24 (pages 2, 12 of PDF). Specific pages cited per quote.

raw_dimension_record.dimension: Source of MTAP-loss MIBC prevalence statistic
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_approach_verbatim_1: Our analysis of the TCGA MIBC cohort showed that approximately 26% of patients harbor homozygous deletion of CDKN2A/CDKN2B/MTAP, while only a minority (approximately 5%) harbor homozygous deletions of only CDKN2A/2B.
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_approach_verbatim_2: Co-occurrence of CDKN2A/2B and MTAP deletions interests 26% of TCGA MIBC patients (p value < 0.001 based on...
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_approach_verbatim_3: Several studies have investigated the molecular landscape of NMIBC and MIBC, revealing that CDKN2A loss at chromosome 9p21 is the most common copy number alteration in BLCA, occurring in approximately 42 and 22% of NMIBC and MIBC, respectively13,14.
raw_dimension_record.CrisPRO_approach: Reproduces and cites Bev2026's 26% figure for MTAP/CDKN2A/CDKN2B co-deletion. The 22% figure (CDKN2A-alone in MIBC, Robertson 2017) is also cited as a complementary endpoint when MTAP-status is not specified. CrisPRO has NOT independently reproduced either number — both are taken from external citations.
raw_dimension_record.moat_status: NEUTRAL — both numbers are Bev2026 + Robertson/Lindskrog original findings. CrisPRO cites them appropriately.
raw_dimension_record.attribution_clarity: Important: 26% (Bev2026 = MTAP+CDKN2A/B co-deletion) ≠ 22% (Robertson 2017 = CDKN2A loss alone, MTAP status not specified). Conflating them would be a methodological error.
raw_dimension_record.verification_status: All three verbatim quotes confirmed by direct PDF read 2026-06-24 (pages 2, 12 of PDF). Specific pages cited per quote.

entities.name: PATH A formula (production ranker)
entities.what_it_solves: Signed decision locking the production ranker formula for CrisPRO to PATH A.
entities.how_it_solves: Approval statement (Fahad Kiani, 2026-04-28): 'Approved PATH A as production ranker formula effective immediately.' Formula: fit = clip((p·t) / ‖t‖₂, 0, 1). Records committed under governance/formula_decision/ in fjkiani/Brenus + fjkiani/crispro-backend-v2.
entities.why_it_exists: Prior sessions oscillated between PATH A and PATH B, quarantining downstream items (DL-03, PC-05, CT-01, Change 1.2). Signing PATH A unblocked ranker recomputation and made PATH B prohibited in all outputs.

entities.name: 8D remediation (approved 2026-04-28)
entities.what_it_solves: Structured 8-Discipline remediation applied to a batch of prior findings.
entities.how_it_solves: Immediate downgrades applied to: filing status, 4-published-trials, LATIFY preliminary label, LATIFY delta quarantine, NCT02264678 split, Claim 16 split, CT-06 §2.4 addendum, script reproducibility (permanent downgrade). Formula-dependent items (CT-01, CT-02, DL-03, DL-07, PC-05) were quarantined until PATH A vs PATH B was resolved.
entities.why_it_exists: Turns a batch of audit findings into a single tracked remediation with a shared timestamp and shared status field.

attributes.pc_02_status: permanently downgraded until retroactive_prediction_run.py --trial-vectors reproduces documented deltas cleanly

entities.name: Mars Protocol — approved knowledge-base sources
entities.what_it_solves: Data governance protocol enumerating which external KBs can be ingested into the SL engine's kb/.
entities.how_it_solves: Approved sources: CIViC (CC0), CGI (CC0), JAX (curated seed), ClinVar (public), DepMap (CC-BY 4.0), PRISM (CC-BY 4.0), GDSC (open access), PubMed/PMC (public). Excluded: OncoKB, Synapse (paywalled), COSMIC, cBioPortal.
entities.why_it_exists: Prevents the pipeline from silently depending on paywalled or license-restricted sources — every claim must be traceable to an open receipt.
